---
title: "401_k_double_ml"
author: "Alexander Unger"
date: "2026-05-22"
output: html_document
---
```{r}

library(OutcomeWeights)  # dml_with_smoother(), get_outcome_weights()
library(cobalt)          # love.plot()
library(viridis)         # colour palette for Love plots
library(gridExtra)       # grid.arrange()
library(ggplot2)         # required by OutcomeWeights


cat(sprintf("BLOCK A ready. N=%d | mean(D)=%.3f | mean(Z)=%.3f\n\n",
            nrow(pension), mean(D), mean(Z)))


# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK A: X matrices for DML
# ==============================================================================
# DML requires a CLEAN numeric matrix without factor attributes.
# grf::validate_X() rejects matrices with lingering factor-level attributes.
# make_X() strips all attributes while preserving column names.
# ncol >= 2 required — single-column matrices fail grf in R 4.5.x.
#
# Card specification for DML (same variables as X_card_kappa, minus intercept):
#   exper, expersq, black, south, smsa, smsa66, reg661–reg668
#   This is a 14-column matrix.
#
# Kitagawa specification for DML (same variables as X_kit_kappa, minus intercept):
#   black, south, smsa, smsa66, south66
#   This is a 5-column matrix.
#
# IMPORTANT: DML X matrices have NO intercept (grf adds it internally).
#            Kappa X matrices (X_card_kappa, X_kit_kappa) retain the intercept
#            column because logit_mle() and cbps() need it.
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK A — DML SETUP FOR 401(k)
# ==============================================================================
# Design:
#   Instrument Z  = e401  (eligibility)
#   Treatment  D  = p401  (participation)
#   Outcome    Y  = net_tfa (net total financial assets)
#   Covariates X  = age, db, educ, fsize, hown, inc, male, marr, pira, twoearn
#
# One covariate spec (unlike Card which had Card + Kitagawa).
# No intercept in DML matrices — grf and DoubleML handle intercepts internally.
# ==============================================================================

make_X <- function(formula, data) {
  # Strips all attributes from model.matrix output to produce a plain numeric
  # matrix safe for grf::validate_X().
  mm <- model.matrix(formula, data = data)
  m  <- matrix(as.numeric(mm), nrow = nrow(mm), ncol = ncol(mm))
  colnames(m) <- colnames(mm)
  m
}

# 401k DML matrix (10 covariates, no intercept)
X_dml_401 <- make_X(
  ~ 0 + age + db + educ + fsize + hown + inc + male + marr + pira + twoearn,
  data = pension
)

cat("DML X matrix:\n")
cat(sprintf("  X_dml_401: %dx%d | numeric=%s\n",
            nrow(X_dml_401), ncol(X_dml_401), is.numeric(X_dml_401)))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions for DML extraction
# (identical in purpose to Card DML Block A)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

get_estimate <- function(res, row_name) {
  # Extract point estimate from OutcomeWeights summary() output.
  if ("Estimate" %in% colnames(res)) return(as.numeric(res[row_name, "Estimate"]))
  as.numeric(res[row_name, 1])
}

check_omega_rows <- function(omega_obj, rows = c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW")) {
  # Verify that the expected outcome-weight rows exist in the omega object.
  missing_rows <- setdiff(rows, rownames(omega_obj$omega))
  if (length(missing_rows) > 0)
    stop("Missing expected outcome-weight rows: ", paste(missing_rows, collapse = ", "))
}

cat("\nBLOCK A complete. Ready for BLOCKS B and C.\n\n")

```
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK B — RUN DOUBLE ML (2-fold and 5-fold cross-fitting)
# ==============================================================================
# dml_with_smoother() from OutcomeWeights runs PLR, PLR-IV, AIPW, Wald-AIPW
# simultaneously and stores the NxN smoother matrices needed for outcome weights.
# Memory note: each dml object stores several NxN matrices.
# With N=9915 this is manageable but remove objects after extracting weights.
# ==============================================================================

set.seed(1234)

cat("Running DML — 2-fold cross-fitting...\n")
dml_2f_401 <- dml_with_smoother(Y, D, X_dml_401, Z, n_cf_folds = 2)
results_dml_2f_401 <- summary(dml_2f_401)
cat("  2-fold results:\n")
print(results_dml_2f_401)

cat("\nExtracting outcome weights (2-fold)...\n")
omega_dml_2f_401 <- get_outcome_weights(dml_2f_401)
check_omega_rows(omega_dml_2f_401, rows = c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"))

# Algebraic identity check: omega'Y should reproduce point estimates exactly
cat("omega'Y replicates point estimates (2-fold)?",
    all.equal(
      as.numeric(omega_dml_2f_401$omega %*% Y),
      as.numeric(results_dml_2f_401[, 1])
    ), "\n")

# Free memory before running 5-fold
rm(dml_2f_401); gc()

cat("\nRunning DML — 5-fold cross-fitting...\n")
dml_5f_401 <- dml_with_smoother(Y, D, X_dml_401, Z, n_cf_folds = 5)
results_dml_5f_401 <- summary(dml_5f_401)
cat("  5-fold results:\n")
print(results_dml_5f_401)

cat("\nExtracting outcome weights (5-fold)...\n")
omega_dml_5f_401 <- get_outcome_weights(dml_5f_401)
check_omega_rows(omega_dml_5f_401, rows = c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"))

cat("omega'Y replicates point estimates (5-fold)?",
    all.equal(
      as.numeric(omega_dml_5f_401$omega %*% Y),
      as.numeric(results_dml_5f_401[, 1])
    ), "\n")

rm(dml_5f_401); gc()

cat("\nBLOCK B complete.\n\n")
```



```{r}

# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK C — COVARIATE BALANCING (love plots)
# ==============================================================================
# Uses cobalt::love.plot() with DML outcome weights.
# Flip sign of untreated weights: multiply by (2*D - 1) to make cobalt-compatible
# (cobalt expects positive treated weights, negative untreated weights flipped).
# ==============================================================================

threshold <- 0.1

create_love_plot_401 <- function(title, index) {
  love.plot(
    D ~ X_dml_401,
    weights = list(
      "2-fold" = omega_dml_2f_401$omega[index, ] * (2 * D - 1),
      "5-fold" = omega_dml_5f_401$omega[index, ] * (2 * D - 1)
    ),
    position   = "bottom",
    title      = title,
    thresholds = c(m = threshold),
    var.order  = "unadjusted",
    binary     = "std",
    abs        = TRUE,
    line       = TRUE,
    colors     = viridis(3),
    shapes     = c("circle", "triangle", "diamond")
  )
}

# Row indices in omega matrix correspond to estimator order from dml_with_smoother:
# 1 = PLR, 2 = PLR-IV, 3 = AIPW, 4 = Wald-AIPW
love_plot_plr_401    <- create_love_plot_401("PLR",       1)
love_plot_plriv_401  <- create_love_plot_401("PLR-IV",    2)
love_plot_aipw_401   <- create_love_plot_401("AIPW",      3)
love_plot_waipw_401  <- create_love_plot_401("Wald-AIPW", 4)

love_plot_plr_401
love_plot_plriv_401
love_plot_aipw_401
love_plot_waipw_401

# Combined plot (mirrors Knaus 2024 Figure 2 structure)
figure_dml_401 <- grid.arrange(
  love_plot_plr_401,   love_plot_aipw_401,
  love_plot_plriv_401, love_plot_waipw_401,
  nrow = 2
)

cat("\nBLOCK C complete.\n\n")






```



```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK D — WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS FOR DML WEIGHTS
# ==============================================================================
# Complements the kappa weight diagnostics from the earlier section.
# Compares DML outcome weights against kappa weights on the same diagnostics:
# Sum_w, ESS, Pct_neg, Max_abs_w.
# ==============================================================================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("DML OUTCOME WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n")

estimator_names <- rownames(omega_dml_2f_401$omega)

cat("\n--- 2-fold cross-fitting ---\n")
tab_2f <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(estimator_names), function(i) {
  weight_diag(omega_dml_2f_401$omega[i, ], estimator_names[i])
}))
print(tab_2f, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n--- 5-fold cross-fitting ---\n")
tab_5f <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(estimator_names), function(i) {
  weight_diag(omega_dml_5f_401$omega[i, ], estimator_names[i])
}))
print(tab_5f, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nBLOCK D complete.\n\n")


```
